form.formComment: Last start was well beaten into eighth behind Showmethetanlines at Menangle over 1609m in a 3yo No NR on March 7 at $34. Raced better the start before that with a game effort when 2nd (Fr2) over 2160m after showing early speed at this track beaten 2 m by The Steel Dragon (2:38.00) in NR 56 to 64 on February 23 at $1.25. Sure to improve on the latest effort.

form.formComment: Comes here second-up after two-and-a-half weeks. Lacklustre first-up performance when surrendered the lead and finished 9th (Fr4) over 2180m after showing early pace at Terang, beaten 40 m by Keayang Renegade (2:37.40) in NR 56 to 64 on March 27 at $15. Hard to have in this line-up.

form.formComment: Last start 5th (Sr2) over 2180m at Stawell, at the bell was 6th-10.9 beaten 10 m by King Kasey (2:39.80) in NR 50 to 55 on April 3 at $61. Two runs back surrendered the lead and finished 7th (Fr2) over 2180m after showing early speed at Terang beaten 35 m by Keayang Renegade (2:37.40) in NR 56 to 64 on March 27 at $101. Would prefer to see a return to form before recommending.

form.formComment: Two back was well beaten into sixth after showing early speed, behind Spook Crocker at Mt Gambier over 2590m in a 4yo+ NR up to 64 on March 14 at $21. Then a handy effort when well beaten into third after being driven well back, behind Keayang Payton at Mt Gambier over 1780m in a NR 45 to 52 on April 7 at $7.50. Can play a role in the finish again.

form.formComment: Last start 4th (Fr4) over 1780m after showing early speed and racing wide at Mt Gambier was 2nd-2.2 at the bell, beaten 11 m by Keayang Payton (2:11.60) in NR 45 to 52 on April 7 at $5. Two runs back posted a big 4 m win as favourite when driven back in the field, after showing early speed, (Fr2) held up early, at Mt Gambier (2:45.10) beating She Will Wantano in 4yo+ NR 46 to 51 on March 14 at $1.85. Capable of playing a major role.

form.formComment: Last start 5th (Sr5) over 1720m at Melton, at the bell was 9th-13.07 beaten 13 m by Tara Victory (2:01.10) in 0W/15 0K stake on March 28 at $126. Before that, was well back in the run when 4th (Fr7) over 2200m at Ballarat beaten 16 m by Heatseeker (2:42.40) in NR 58 to 67 on March 19 at $11. Certainly worth another chance.

previousStarts.stewardsComment: Tired over the concluding stages to be beaten 83m into 12th place.

Hung in under pressure rounding the final turn.
Pulled hard whilst racing outside the leader.
